question: Which of the following factors is associated with a *decreased* delay in the adoption of hearing aids for adults who are candidates for amplification?
source_title: Time From Hearing Aid Candidacy to Hearing Aid Adoption: A Longitudinal Cohort Study.

options.label: A
options.text: Better speech recognition ability in difficult listening tasks.

options.label: B
options.text: Belonging to a nonwhite racial group.

options.label: C
options.text: A higher degree of self-assessed hearing handicap in social situations.

options.label: D
options.text: Having a less severe degree of hearing loss.

question: True or False: Children with mild-to-moderate hearing loss show significant overall deficits in voice emotion recognition compared to their normally hearing peers.
source_title: Voice Emotion Recognition by Children With Mild-to-Moderate Hearing Loss.

question: For bimodal patients, what is the recommended approach for fitting the hearing aid (HA) bandwidth to achieve the best bimodal performance?
reference_answer: Fit the HA with the widest bandwidth consistent with effective audibility.

question: In Mandarin-speaking children with cochlear implants (CIs), most domains of health-related quality of life (HRQoL) show rapid progress in the first 3 months of CI use. Which domain progresses at a slower rate?
source_title: Health-Related Quality of Life in Mandarin-Speaking Children With Cochlear Implants.

question: For mid-adults with normal hearing who report speech-in-noise difficulties, what two assessments should be included as part of a routine clinical evaluation to better understand these complaints?
reference_answer: Assessment of extended high-frequency (EHF) hearing and working memory.

question: For optimal preservation of intracochlear soft tissue structures during cochlear implant (CI) surgery, which surgical approach is recommended over a cochleostomy?
source_title: Effects of Various Trajectories on Tissue Preservation in Cochlear Implant Surgery: A Micro-Computed Tomography and Synchrotron Radiation Phase-Contrast Imaging Study.

question: Among the Big-Five personality traits, which one was found to moderately influence post-implantation hearing-related quality of life (hrQoL) in postlingually deafened adults?

question: True or False: Cortical-level auditory stream segregation is unaltered among individuals with tinnitus compared to those without.

options.text: Reversal of maladaptive plasticity in the central auditory system.

options.label: B
options.text: Masking of the tinnitus via the sound from the implant.

options.label: C
options.text: Improved speech perception, which distracts from the tinnitus.

options.label: D
options.text: Psychological acceptance of the tinnitus due to improved quality of life.

question: True or False: When listening to spectrally degraded phonemes, children and adults benefit equally from a reduction in simulated channel interaction, with performance for both groups plateauing at the same point.

options.label: A
options.text: Dual-task visual reaction times (RTs).

options.label: B
options.text: Verbal reaction times (RTs).

options.label: C
options.text: Subjective rating scales.

options.label: D
options.text: Both visual and verbal RTs are equally sensitive.

question: True or False: User self-adjustment of basic hearing aid amplification characteristics is only feasible and effective for individuals who have prior hearing aid experience.

question: For listeners with sensorineural hearing loss (SNHL) using hearing aids, how does amplification affect the contribution of high frequencies to overall loudness perception compared to listeners with normal hearing (NH)?

question: In symphony orchestra musicians, what is the most important factor associated with both the prevalence and severity of tinnitus, even in those without hearing loss?
source_title: Tinnitus Severity Is Related to the Sound Exposure of Symphony Orchestra Musicians Independently of Hearing Impairment.

question: When comparing "Next-Generation" auditory steady state response (ASSR) techniques to auditory brainstem response (ABR) with automated detection, which method was found to be significantly faster for predicting an audiogram?

question: In young infants (birth to 8 weeks), clinical interpretation of distortion product otoacoustic emissions (DPOAEs) is most predictive in which frequency region?
source_title: Optimizing Clinical Interpretation of Distortion Product Otoacoustic Emissions in Infants.

options.label: A
options.text: 1 to 1.5 kHz.

options.label: B
options.text: 2 to 8 kHz.

options.label: C
options.text: Below 1 kHz.

options.label: D
options.text: Across all frequencies equally.

question: True or False: Apparent deficits in temporal resolution, as measured by stimulus-level thresholds for gap detection, are dependent on age and audibility.
source_title: The Influence of Hearing Aid Gain on Gap-Detection Thresholds for Children and Adults With Hearing Loss.

question: For 6- to 10-year-old children with cochlear implants (CIs), what aspect of learning new words may be more challenging than comprehension, but is consistent with their overall vocabulary size?

question: For adult cochlear implant (CI) recipients with asymmetric hearing, improvements in sound localization and self-reported communication benefit after implantation are significant and are not related to what factor?
reference_answer: The amount of hearing in the better ear.
source_title: Results in Adult Cochlear Implant Recipients With Varied Asymmetric Hearing: A Prospective Longitudinal Study of Speech Recognition, Localization, and Participant Report.

question: In infants with normal hearing, distortion product otoacoustic emission (DPOAE) levels are related to maturational changes in what part of the auditory system?
source_title: Longitudinal Development of Distortion Product Otoacoustic Emissions in Infants With Normal Hearing.

question: True or False: The ability to take advantage of vocal characteristic differences between male and female talkers for speech-in-speech perception is fully developed in infancy.

question: True or False: For a listener with bilateral hearing devices, spatial release from masking (SRM) is affected by both unilateral frequency shifts (creating interaural mismatch) and bilateral frequency shifts (maintaining interaural match).

question: After adjusting for otoacoustic emissions and sex, a reduced amplitude of which auditory brainstem response (ABR) wave was associated with an increased risk of tinnitus?
source_title: Tinnitus and Auditory Perception After a History of Noise Exposure: Relationship to Auditory Brainstem Response Measures.

question: Besides thresholds in noise, what two auditory brainstem response (ABR) measures may be useful for the prediction of hidden hearing loss (HHL) in humans?
reference_answer: The summating potential to action potential (SP/AP) ratio and ABR wave I amplitude.

question: Compared to hearing nonsigners, decreased brain activation in several regions during mental rotation tasks in deaf signers is thought to reflect what two effects of long-term sign language experience?

reference_answer: Increased phase locking, increased amplitude, and decreased latency.
source_title: Effects of Amplification on Neural Phase Locking, Amplitude, and Latency to a Speech Syllable.

question: Which f2 frequency shows minimal maturational effects and measurement error, suggesting it may be an optimal sentinel frequency for ototoxicity monitoring in pediatric patients using distortion-product otoacoustic emissions (DPOAEs)?
source_title: Long-Term Variability of Distortion-Product Otoacoustic Emissions in Infants and Children and Its Relation to Pediatric Ototoxicity Monitoring.

question: True or False: In older, postlingually deaf adults, core speech perception outcomes after cochlear implantation are related to several underlying foundational neurocognitive abilities.

question: In older adults, what sensory modality may play an important compensatory role for maintaining cognitive ability when hearing impairment sets in?

question: True or False: For children with normal hearing or mild/unilateral hearing loss, performance on challenging multi-talker audiovisual tasks is highly dependent on maintaining visual fixation on the relevant talker.

question: For children with early cochlear implants (CIs), having bilateral CIs predicted significantly better outcomes in what specific area of psychosocial development?

question: What component of the electrocochleography (ECochG) response, when recorded directly from the contacts of a cochlear implant (CI) during surgery, can be used to access the intracochlear frequency position of the CI?
source_title: The Summating Potential Is a Reliable Marker of Electrode Position in Electrocochleography: Cochlear Implant as a Theragnostic Probe.

question: True or False: Electrically evoked compound action potential (eCAP) thresholds are precise enough to reliably predict T-level profiles for cochlear implant programming.
source_title: The Precision of eCAP Thresholds Derived From Amplitude Growth Functions.

question: True or False: For listeners with hearing loss, relevant information for normalizing the perceived loudness of binaural broadband signals can be accurately inferred from monaural narrowband loudness functions.
source_title: Restoring Perceived Loudness for Listeners With Hearing Loss.

question: In adult cochlear implant (CI) recipients, what intervention provides a remarkable benefit for tests of low-frequency (LF) pitch perception and challenging speech perception that rely on temporal fine structure (TFS) cues?
reference_answer: Use of a contralateral hearing aid (bimodal stimulation).

question: True or False: The Flemish Digit Triplet Test (DTT) with broadband noise has high sensitivity and specificity for detecting high-frequency hearing impairment in a middle-aged population.
source_title: Digit Triplet Test Hearing Screening With Broadband and Low-Pass Filtered Noise in a Middle-Aged Population.

question: When using the smartphone digits-in-noise test as a hearing screening tool in a multilingual population, what factor had a significant influence on the speech reception threshold (SRT) obtained?
source_title: The South African English Smartphone Digits-in-Noise Hearing Test: Effect of Age, Hearing Loss, and Speaking Competence.

question: Underlying delays and deficits in what two areas may place some long-term cochlear implant (CI) users at risk for difficulties in psychosocial adjustment?

question: True or False: Children with otitis media with effusion (OME)-related hearing loss may develop compensatory strategies that reduce the effects of the hearing loss in adverse listening environments.

question: Guided internet-based Cognitive Behavior Therapy (iCBT) for tinnitus resulted in significant reductions in tinnitus distress and what four common comorbidities?

source_title: Audiologist-Guided Internet-Based Cognitive Behavior Therapy for Adults With Tinnitus in the United Kingdom: A Randomized Controlled Trial.

question: True or False: Self-reported listening effort and fatigue correlate with hearing handicap but not with objective speech recognition scores.

question: In a college-aged population, what two general categories of factors can trigger tinnitus perception?

question: In young children with cochlear implants (CIs), what factor mitigates the development of early signs of psychopathology?
source_title: Terrible Twos or Early Signs of Psychopathology? Developmental Patterns in Early Identified Preschoolers With Cochlear Implants Compared With Hearing Controls.

question: True or False: In experienced unilateral, bilateral, and bimodal cochlear implant (CI) users, performance on the Spectral-Temporally Modulated Ripple Test (SMRT) is strongly associated with speech recognition in noise ability.
source_title: Comparison of the Spectral-Temporally Modulated Ripple Test With the Arizona Biomedical Institute Sentence Test in Cochlear Implant Users.

question: Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT) was associated with significant improvements in patients with chronic, distressing tinnitus. These changes were associated with increases in what two psychological factors?
source_title: Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy for Chronic Tinnitus: Evaluation of Benefits in a Large Sample of Patients Attending a Tinnitus Clinic.

question: True or False: Bilateral cochlear implant (CI) listeners have a narrow range of binaural pitch fusion, similar to that of normal-hearing listeners.
source_title: Binaural Pitch Fusion in Bilateral Cochlear Implant Users.

question: How strong is the evidence supporting the use of electrically evoked compound action potential (eCAP) thresholds for predicting T- and C-levels for cochlear implant fitting?
source_title: Use of Electrically Evoked Compound Action Potentials for Cochlear Implant Fitting: A Systematic Review.

options.label: A
options.text: There is very strong evidence, making it a gold standard for fitting.

options.label: B
options.text: There is weak evidence, as eCAP thresholds are an equally weak predictor for both T- and C-levels.

options.label: C
options.text: There is strong evidence for predicting T-levels, but weak evidence for C-levels.

options.label: D
options.text: There is strong evidence for predicting C-levels, but weak evidence for T-levels.

question: True or False: The effects of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) on cortical auditory-evoked potential (CAEP) amplitudes and latencies in infants can be accurately predicted from adult data.

question: In cochlear implant sound coding strategies that use simultaneous dual-channel stimulation, how does the distance between the two channels affect the amount of current needed to achieve a loudness match to a single channel?

question: To ensure that the Distortion Product Otoacoustic Emission (DPOAE) response is unaffected by other electrophysiologic testing, what is the recommended order for performing DPOAE and Auditory Brainstem Response (ABR) assessments?
source_title: Temporary reduction of distortion product otoacoustic emissions (DPOAEs) immediately following auditory brainstem response (ABR).

options.label: A
options.text: The order does not matter.

options.label: B
options.text: ABR assessment should be performed before DPOAE measurement.

options.label: C
options.text: DPOAE measurement should be performed before ABR assessment.

options.label: D
options.text: The tests should be performed on separate days.

question: When measuring ear-canal power reflectance and Distortion Product Otoacoustic Emissions (DPOAEs), in which postural position is intra-subject variability generally smaller (i.e., more stable)?
source_title: Posture systematically alters ear-canal reflectance and DPOAE properties.

options.label: A
options.text: Tilted at -45 degrees

options.label: B
options.text: Lying supine

options.label: C
options.text: Upright at 90 degrees

options.label: D
options.text: Variability is the same regardless of posture
